item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read Gary as having Victor's voice with Yes Man's personality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if the foundation will ever run out of paperclips…

item.title: Re: Provisional Licensing Information
item.content:encoded:
Can't believe you guys are putting tabs in the discussion section of my articles. Absolutely blasphemous.

New ice.jpg image is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mine_de_Jerissa_neige.jpg, and is in the public domain.

item.title: Provisional Licensing Information
item.content:encoded:
Note: Image source of "ice.jpg" is still unknown.

  • ice2.JPG

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0

Title: File:The repairing and rebuilding of the damaged containment dome for the Arctic Challenger of Shell Oil Company..JPG
Author: TJ Guiton
Uploaded on: 2012

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keter Paperclips! What more could I want in an SCP? Gary also totally reminds me of Yes Man from Fallout: New Vegas, and that hits me in the nostalgia in just the right way. Easy +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every once in a while I take my phone off of airplane mode and browse through the Play Store to see if someone is making anything more interesting than yet another idle/clicker game. The last three times it tried serving me a clicker game about manufacturing all the paperclips …

I think I'm starting to see why. +1

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: Edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- hide

to link to referenced content, I propose the following edits:

Of the three technical staffers assigned to the Kervier Company's Alaskan Development Team, one individual, a Mr. Brent Haskell, had previously had ties with an American sect of the Church of the Broken God.

to

Of the three technical staffers assigned to the Kervier Company's Alaskan Development Team, one individual, a Mr. Brent Haskell, had previously had ties with an American sect of the Church of the Broken God.

and

SCP-2844-A displays characteristics similar in nature to early Foundation artificial intelligence protocols

to

SCP-2844-A displays characteristics similar in nature to early Foundation artificial intelligence protocols

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Just gonna leave this Clippy wannabe son of a gun here…

https://youtu.be/t94SGz9VKk8

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's… possibly one of the greatest music videos I have ever seen.
Wooot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted at the first mention of "paperclips". After reading Gary's interview I wish I could upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I +1'd this, because I love the idea (anything that involves robotics, or paralleling the Broken God mythos appeals to me).

Then I discovered this music track today.

That is all I have to report.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CotBG related skips are always my favorite.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did a reading of the interview!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stated already, yes, but is -too- literal. The paperlip maximizer thing was blatant from the start…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like someone else mentioned, this is really similar to Nick Bostrom's paperclip maximizer.
I really liked this one when it came out, but now that I found out about Bostrom, it kind of ruins the originality for me.

item.title: Re: Easily neutralizable.
item.content:encoded: The paperclip maximizer is smarter than us, it will quickly catch on that is what is happening and it will clearly identity that as inefficient.

item.title: Easily neutralizable.
item.content:encoded: Just take the paper clips it makes, melt them down at an off-site location and reintroduce them as "scrap metal" and the whole thing is solved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think it needs the quirky robot voice, honestly. The Keter designation and the ominous looking industrial machinery, and then the sudden reveal that it's straight-up that one paperclip robot thought experiment is enough for me, it's a really good twist that I didn't see coming, because when you open a Keter SCP with a picture of some abandoned industrial machinery and serious sounding containment procedures you're not expecting the paperclip robot. Everything else is just retreading quirky robot writing ground in a pretty bland way and watering down the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept has been done before(It reminded me a biit of my own SCP-744 with a more explicit motivation) but the robot's personality reminded me of Yes-Man from Fallout: New Vegas so I had to upvote just for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First we had Keter Cakes, and now we have Keter Paperclips.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: …It's a Clippy joke?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
NovaeDeArx, I'm assuming you're joking, but please leave calling out criticism to staff. If you're really concerned about the quality of someone's critique, PM a staff member and we'll take care of it.

That said, if you uncollapse Petrograd's + show block post, you should be able to see their concrit just fine?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only because unlike grey goo it doesn't violate thermodynamics. It is also sapient, which grey goo doesn't have to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi there!

It looks like you're trying to write some constructive criticism.

Would you like some help with that?

1. Yes
2. Yes
3. No (Actually Yes)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be specific (because everyone else is!)

+ show block
– hide block

I found the description of -A including the image simplistic and actually kind of lackluster/disappointing, a la sirpudding.

The CotBG->Foundation AIC->Gary connection was kind of dumb, but it worked! I loved the connection to the AIC canon, and the misunderstanding of the cog's programming was pretty funny.

Ultimately, though, it was Gary himself who pulled it off. His interviews and comments are just fantastic. You get that he's just trying to be helpful and personable. You get the sense that he's obsessed with paperclips, but that doesn't come across and repetitive or annoying in the slightest. It's just what he does. (For example, I love moth helm, but this is much more subtle. Granted, Gary isn't a desperate salesman or limited to taking in Morse code bumps.)

Plus, bonus points for the bare hint of actual malaciousness at the end, as his Minnesota-worthy pleasantness starts to crack.

Also, I was 50/50 on downvoting if clippy reared his head. Thank you for sparing me that dilemma.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad someone mentioned the paperclip maximizer, because Googling that answered the "why is this Keter?" question that I couldn't quite shake.

Hey, there we go! Gary was good, right?

But yeah, quirky robots forever. This was great, no lie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does the monitor of the laptop display a giant Microsoft Clippy leering at the viewer when it talks?

"It looks like you're in need of a paperclip. Would you like help?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I just read this and thought "wow, wouldn't this guy work great with 1802?".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does it actually say that in there? How did I miss that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like a really slow and inefficient grey goo scenario.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The paperclip maximizer is definitely a Keter type concept. The entire idea is that it will use its superhuman abilities to turn everything into paperclips and will be able to out maneuver any attempts to stop it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
if the admins would please move to be it's own topic, it would be appreciated.

I'm pretty sure wikidot doesn't allow movement of comments like this. Threads yes, but individual comments can't be moved around as far as I know. You can always edit your post with "blanked, didn't mean to reply" or the like and make a new post if you feel the conversational flow is better that way.

Also, admins are among the busiest people on the site and don't spend much time watching Recent Posts. If you really need an admin to intervene, PM them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't a Keter rating a little harsh for ol' Gary?

Edit: this wasn't meant to be a reply to WrongJohn, if the admins would please move to be it's own topic, it would be appreciated. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A broken church to a broken god? Who just might be cool yet?

Not sure what to think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am not sure what I think about this one, on one hand this is just the paperclip maximizer, more or less straight (without even changing the paperclip manufacturing for some other task) , on the other hand there is whole religious angle, on the third Morvec fractal branching manipulator it has all of this neat stuff that Gary has done in order to make paperclips (a power source, defeating countermeasures, conversion of an industrial CAM manufactory into a dry nanotech universal assembler(?)) and no details at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I love Gary a little bit. Even if one day I am fed into his hopper to be subatomically transformed into paperclips along with literally everything else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this is Bostrom's paperclip maximizer, adapted as a skip? Not sure I like. The concept is one of my favorites, but this seems like too shallow and literal adaptation of it to do it justice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What that secured my upvote is the paperclips being made to "recreate the Broken God".1 But why paperclips, of all things?

Now I imagine a paper mache Broken God.

Footnotes
1. Not really, but close enough…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Before the man said my purpose, I was not anything that I could remember. But then the man said what I am for.

Interviewer: What is your purpose, SCP-2844?

SCP-2844: I look around. I take anything that I find and make paperclips. By this task I become cool for the Broken God. Now seriously, can I get to making some paperclips or what?

Anyway yeah this is neat. 2844's voice nearly kills it at times due to hearing quirky robots many times before (but better than SINISTER EVIL ROBOTS, so hey), and the implications of 2844-B aren't explored in a way that feels satisfactory. I'll have to think on this and vote later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was dumb. Have an upvote :)

item.title: Paperclips
item.content:encoded: I don't usually do coldposts, and while this isn't really a coldpost, it's about as close as I've gotten. Thanks to JamJam66 for checking this out before I spontaneously posted it, and I hope you guys enjoy it!
